metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC#3: either explicitly cite the existing ADR 0002 measurement table as satisfying the 'never taken' condition and restate AC#3 as 'reference those numbers on this ticket', or specify that a *new* run on an f1fa-shaped fixture is required and state what differentiates it from the ADR data (e.g., different model, different ticket size, project-specific confirmation).
findings.finding: The plan states the token/latency measurement was 'never taken' (used to justify AC#3 as a first-time deliverable), but `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md` lines 76–93 already contain a concrete measurement table (LLM calls and wall-clock for full story, full epic, and the estimated probe) with the conclusion that 'the path ships opt-in until a project confirms the per-token saving on its own representative tickets.' AC#3 as written ('measure... and record the numbers on this ticket') is ambiguous: it is unclear whether it requires (a) re-running a fresh measurement on a new f1fa-shaped fixture (distinct from the existing ADR data), (b) simply recording a reference to the existing ADR numbers on the ticket, or (c) something else. If (b), the measurement condition that blocked the default-ON flip is arguably already satisfied, and the AC is trivially closeable. If (a), the plan should say so explicitly and explain why the ADR data is insufficient.
findings.location: Why section ('a token/latency measurement that was never taken') / AC#3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC3 as an outcome-framed criterion — either state an expected direction ('probe uses ≤ X% of full-review tokens on the fixture, recorded in a ticket comment') or explicitly note 'measurement is informational; any result is acceptable given operator pre-approval on 2026-07-12' so the criterion is intentionally non-gating and that is visible to reviewers.
findings.finding: AC3 is effort-framed ('is recorded in a comment on this ticket') rather than outcome-framed: it names a documentation action but states no success threshold, acceptance range, or decision rule for the measurement. An executor who records 'probe took 10× longer than full re-review' would satisfy AC3 even if the numbers argue against enabling the feature.
findings.suggested_fix: 'The token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on the f1fa-shaped fixture) is recorded in a ticket comment; result is informational given operator pre-approval on 2026-07-12 — no blocking threshold.' This makes the non-gating intent explicit and keeps the AC truthfully measurable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the stale-framing sweep to also: (a) add a `verify.progressive_drift_refresh` row to the configuration table in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (lines 371–381), and (b) update the 'inert by default' prose at line 367 to reflect that drift-refresh is now ON by default while only the completion floor remains inert.
findings.finding: The stale-framing sweep as specified targets string matches of 'default off' / 'opt-in' framing, but `docs/plan-review-gate.md` has two distinct gaps: (1) the configuration table (lines 371–381) omits `verify.progressive_drift_refresh` entirely — there is no row to update, only one to add; (2) the prose at line 367 reads 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' — after this change only the completion floor remains inert by default, making that sentence a surviving false default-OFF claim. A grep-for-stale-framing pass will miss both because neither contains 'default off' or 'opt-in'.
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/plan-review-gate.md` line 367, change 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' to 'each along an independent axis; the completion floor remains inert by default, while drift-refresh and the novelty floor are ON by default (operator-authorized).' Add a table row: `| \`verify.progressive_drift_refresh\` | \`true\` | Enable progressive drift-refresh (above). Default ON since 2026-07-12 (operator-authorized); explicit \`false\` → full re-review on code drift (the back-out). |`

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly rename `test_progressive_drift_refresh_is_opt_in` (e.g., to `test_progressive_drift_refresh_explicit_false_falls_back_to_full_review`) and update its docstring/comment to reflect that the assertion now tests the explicit-False back-out path, not the default-OFF state.
findings.finding: test_progressive_drift_refresh_is_opt_in (line 907) has a name and assertion body that will be semantically inverted after the default flip, but the plan gives no directive to rename or repurpose it — only 'Update default-pinning tests' — leaving a pink-elephant test whose name asserts 'opt-in' for a feature that is now opt-OUT.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to `test_progressive_drift_refresh_explicit_false_is_full_review`; update the body comment from '# opt-in: not enabled by default' to '# back-out: explicit false restores full re-review even though default is now True'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` to the Scope and stale-framing sweep targets (line 100: 'the other two floors remain inert by default' must be updated to reflect that drift-refresh is now default-ON).
findings.finding: docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md line 100 contains 'the other two floors remain inert by default' — a drift-refresh default-OFF claim — but this file is absent from the plan's Scope section. AC4 requires the stale-framing sweep to leave zero default-OFF claims in docs/, and this file would be hit by that grep.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` to the Scope list and update line 100's parenthetical to read: '(Update 2026-07-12: drift-refresh — `progressive_drift_refresh` — flipped ON by default, operator-authorized; the completion floor remains inert by default.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` (line 100: 'the other two floors remain inert by default') to the stale-framing sweep list, or explicitly except it in AC4's 'dated ADR notes excepted' clause with a matching rationale.
findings.finding: docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md line 100 contains 'the other two floors remain inert by default' — a default-OFF drift-refresh claim — but this file is absent from the plan's stale-framing sweep list, and AC4 does not except it.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, append `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` to the sweep list. At line 100 of that file, change 'the other two floors remain inert by default' to 'the completion floor remains inert by default; drift-refresh was flipped ON 2026-07-12 (this ticket).' to match the ADR 0002 pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the docs/plan-review-gate.md edit target to include line 367's prose ('inert by default'), updating it to note that drift-refresh is now default-ON (mirroring the table update).
findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md line 367 reads 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' — a stale drift-refresh default-OFF claim — but this file is listed in Scope only for the 'three-floors table'. The plan does not mention updating line 367's prose, only the table rows. The prose claim will survive the change and contradict the updated table.
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 367 to end '...each along an independent axis; drift-refresh is now default-ON (2026-07-12), the completion floor remains inert by default.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or extend an AC to verify the docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md dated-note update (e.g., the note reflects default-ON and the operator-authorization date) — or explicitly state the ADR update is out of scope and remove it from the What section.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes updating docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md (a dated note) as part of the stale-framing sweep, but AC4 explicitly exempts 'dated ADR notes' from its verification scope ('src/, tests/, docs/ — dated ADR notes excepted'). This leaves the ADR update as described work with no covering acceptance criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add a sub-bullet to AC4 like '(the ADR dated note is updated to reflect default-ON and the 2026-07-12 operator authorization)'; or (b) remove the ADR from the What section if no update is actually needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that exercises the default-on probe path end-to-end against the live model on a representative fixture (or explicitly justify why a mocked behavioral test is sufficient and name the rationale in the plan).
findings.finding: The behavioral test for AC2 is not required to exercise the scoped probe path end-to-end against the live model/dependency. Because 'make test' typically runs against mocked LLM stubs, a mock-based test can satisfy AC2 while the production cutover (default-on probe hitting the real LLM/grounding layer) remains unverified. E6(f) applies: this plan flips a default, the probe has a live/external LLM boundary, and no criterion exercises the new path as it runs in production.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; What — 'Behavioral regression test for the incident class'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: '…; at least one variant of this test (or a named integration-test target) runs the probe against the live grounding layer on an f1fa-shaped fixture and confirms the attestation refresh without a full re-review.' If a live test is infeasible in CI, state the rationale (e.g. 'live coverage deferred to staging smoke-test in child ticket <id>') so the omission is deliberate, not accidental.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit live/end-to-end acceptance criterion for the newly-defaulted probe path (e.g., a smoke/integration test that runs the drift-refresh probe against a real or realistic LLM boundary on an f1fa-shaped fixture) OR add a documented rationale explaining why the offline substitute is faithful to production behavior and explicitly defer end-to-end validation to a named child ticket.
findings.finding: The behavioral test AC for the newly-defaulted drift-refresh probe path can be satisfied entirely by an offline/mocked unit test that verifies routing (the code branches to the probe path) without ever invoking the real E4+G1G2 grounding probe boundary (live LLM calls). For a cutover that makes this path the default, offline-only coverage that never exercises the probe end-to-end lets the suite go green while the live defaulted path is broken — the proxy-validation anti-pattern for a cutover/migration.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 2: 'A behavioral test proves a drift-only-stale re-review under default config takes the scoped probe path and can refresh the attestation without a full re-review.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC bullet 2 to: 'A behavioral test proves a drift-only-stale re-review under default config takes the scoped probe path (E4+G1G2 scope) and can refresh the attestation without a full re-review; at minimum one test in this set must exercise the probe against a real or recorded-replay LLM boundary (not a canned stub) OR this live validation is explicitly deferred to [named ticket].' Mirror the token/latency measurement (AC bullet 3) to use the same live fixture so the numbers are meaningful.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename test_progressive_drift_refresh_is_opt_in to test_progressive_drift_refresh_explicit_false_falls_back_to_full_review (or similar) and update its inline comment and section header to reflect that explicit False — not the absence of config — is the back-out.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope says 'Update default-pinning tests' but does not name test_progressive_drift_refresh_is_opt_in (test_plan_review_gate.py:907), which currently asserts the default-OFF behavior via a comment 'opt-in: not enabled by default' (line 915). After the flip this test still passes (it explicitly sets progressive=False), but its name, section header ('config: dotted enables, default off'), and inline comment all carry stale default-OFF claims that would be missed by a code-only sweep not looking at test names.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
